message:
🌱 Add podman-desktop: Remote management of podman hosts mission (#1187)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "podman-desktop-279-remote-management-of-podman-hosts",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "podman-desktop: Remote management of podman hosts",
+    "description": "Remote management of podman hosts. Requested by 15+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current podman-desktop deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your podman-desktop version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
helm list -n podman-desktop 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working podman-desktop installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review podman-desktop configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant podman-desktop configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl get configmap -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=podman-desktop
```
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

I can't seem to find a way to connect to a remote machine. This would be a great feature in place of a web GUI running on the remote host, like Portainer."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Remote management of podman hosts",
+        "description": "### What does this PR do?

It adds a couple of functions to detect if the user is on WSL or HyperV. This will be in handy in future if someone wants to show some properties/settings based on the virtualization used on Windows. This allows to use these properties on the when clauses or from external
```yaml
[
    {
        "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
        "name": "media",
        "plugins": [
            {
                "bridge": "cni-podman0",
                "hairpinMode": true,
                "ipMasq": true,
                "ipam": {
                    "ranges": [
                        [
                            {
                                "gateway": "10.88.2.1",
                                "subnet": "10.88.2.0/24"
                            }
                        ]
                    ],
                    "routes": [
                        {
                           
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n podman-desktop -l app.kubernetes.io/name=podman-desktop
kubectl get events -n podman-desktop --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Remote management of podman hosts" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "### What does this PR do?

It adds a couple of functions to detect if the user is on WSL or HyperV. This will be in handy in future if someone wants to show some properties/settings based on the virtualization used on Windows.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "[
    {
        "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
        "name": "media",
        "plugins": [
            {
                "bridge": "cni-podman0",
                "hairpinMode": true,
                "ipMasq": true,
                "ipam": {
                    "ranges": [
                        [
                            {
                                "gateway": "10.88.2.1",
                                "subnet": "10.88.2.0/24"
                            }
                        ]
                    ],
                    "routes": [
                        {
                            "dst": "0.0.0.0/0"
                        }
                    ],
                    "type": "host-local"
                },
                "isGateway": true,
                "type": "bridge"
   ",
+        "#!/bin/bash

podname="media"
network="media"

podman pod rm -f ${podname}
podman pod create --name ${podname} --network ${network} -p 7979:7979

podman run --name=radarr -d --pod=${podname} -e UID=1000 -e GID=1000 -e TZ=America/Somecity-e UMASK_SET=022 -v /path/to/radarr/data:/config -v /path/to:/media-v /path/to/downloads:/downloads --restart unless-stopped lscr.io/linuxserver/radarr:latest"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
